text: The surgeon introduces an endobag into the abdominal cavity, rolling it open and positioning it within the pelvis. The ovary containing the dermoid cyst is carefully placed inside the endobag to prevent spillage into the peritoneal cavity.

The left ovary is observed to have a 4x3 cm dermoid cyst. The surgeon uses cold knife scissors to create a dissection plane over the ovarian cortex at its most prominent point. The cyst wall is then gently opened and extended using a laparoscopic shearer.

The surgeon performs enucleation of the dermoid cyst from the ovarian cortex using gentle traction and counter traction, ensuring the cyst does not rupture or spill its contents outside the endobag. The cystectomy is executed along the correct plane of dissection within the endobag.

The surgeon completes the bloodless dissection of the dermoid cyst within the endobag. The cyst contents are carefully spilled open inside the bag to prevent spillage into the peritoneum. Suction aspiration is used to aspirate the contents from the bag. After complete aspiration, the cystectomy is finalized, and the ovarian bed is examined for any bleeding or spurting vessels.

The small cyst is visible inside the endobag and is being retrieved through a ten mm suprapubic port. A gentle peritoneal lavage is performed, followed by re-aspiration to remove any spilled contents. The ovarian bed is shown with complete hemostasis, without any bleeding.
